title: Underwater Dance Choreographer
description: The user choreographs an underwater dance sequence where Michelle performs synchronized movements with a school of BarramundiFish orbiting around her. Users place animation keyframes on a timeline, adjust fish orbit parameters, couple Michelle's dance speed to fish velocity, and use undo/redo to refine the performance while maintaining a conservation law on total kinetic energy across all animated entities.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 that implements an underwater dance choreography editor. Load Michelle.glb at the scene center standing on a subtle sandy plane, and load BarramundiFish.glb, instancing it 5 times in a circular orbit around Michelle. Use a blue-tinted fog, a directional light from above simulating caustics, and an ambient light for underwater atmosphere. Set the camera at position (0, 2, 6) looking at Michelle with OrbitControls enabled.

Implement these interactions: (1) A horizontal timeline bar at the bottom with 8 slots where users click to place keyframes. (2) Each keyframe opens a small panel to set Michelle's pose blend weight between her available animation clips (idle, walk, etc.) using a slider. (3) A radial slider controls the fish orbit radius (1-4 units). (4) A speed slider controls fish orbital velocity (0.5-3 rad/s). (5) Michelle's animation playback speed is coupled to fish velocity via a multiplier the user sets (0.5x-2x). (6) Enforce a total kinetic energy conservation law: when fish speed increases, Michelle's animation speed must proportionally decrease unless the user explicitly adjusts the coupling multiplier. Display the total energy as a numeric HUD value that must remain constant. (7) A play/pause button previews the choreography. (8) Full undo/redo stack (Ctrl+Z/Ctrl+Y) for all parameter changes, supporting at least 20 history states. (9) A reset button restores defaults. (10) Clicking a placed keyframe allows deletion or modification.

Use AnimationMixer for Michelle's clips and procedural rotation for fish orbits. Render fish with slight vertical sine-wave bobbing. Show the current keyframe index and energy value in an overlay. All UI should be HTML/CSS overlays, not 3D widgets.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  fishOrbitRadius: number,         // current radius of fish circular orbit in units (1-4)
  fishOrbitalVelocity: number,         // angular velocity of fish orbit in radians per second (0.5-3)
  michelleAnimationSpeed: number,         // playback speed multiplier for Michelle's animation (0.1-3)
  couplingMultiplier: number,         // user-set multiplier linking fish velocity to Michelle speed (0.5-2)
  totalKineticEnergy: number,         // computed sum of fish orbital energy and Michelle animation energy
  activeKeyframeIndex: number,         // index of currently selected timeline keyframe slot (0-7, -1 if none)
  keyframeCount: number,         // total number of keyframes placed on timeline (0-8)
  michellePoseBlendWeight: number,         // blend weight for Michelle's current animation pose (0-1)
  undoStackDepth: number,         // number of actions available in undo history (0-20)
  redoStackDepth: number,         // number of actions available in redo history (0-20)
  timelinePlayheadPosition: number,         // normalized position of playhead on timeline (0-1)
  isCouplingActive: boolean,         // whether fish-Michelle speed coupling is currently enforced
}
